title: Purged race-level validation with embargo and novelty checks on unseen drivers
summary: The dataset has repeated rows within races and many drivers repeated across years; naive folds can overestimate ROC AUC when future race structure bleeds into training.
rationale: The dataset has repeated rows within races and many drivers repeated across years; naive folds can overestimate ROC AUC when future race structure bleeds into training. Purged/embargo CV is a standard defense for event-driven sequences, and this competition likely needs race-blocked validation to avoid false feature wins.
implementation_hint: Compare current CV against stricter splits such as `GroupKFold` by `(Year,Race)`, leave-one-race-out within year, and a purged variant that drops neighboring laps/races around validation groups. Track feature gains only if they survive stricter CV. Also report separate fold metrics for unseen drivers/races appearing only in test-era seasons.
expected_effect: May not raise local CV, but should improve leaderboard fidelity and reduce wasted iterations on leaky ideas; can change which hypotheses are actually worth keeping.
risk: CV score may drop materially, and overly harsh splits can become high-variance with only 26 races.
